introduction: This spec defines workspace management and workspace token delegation for af-hub, covering CRUD endpoints for workspaces and their associated access tokens. Note: requirements referencing server_foundation (spec 01) auth middleware and oauth_and_users (spec 02) identity APIs are based on PRD-described interfaces and should be cross-checked against the installed implementations before coding.

glossary.DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:: HTTP endpoint for permanently revoking a workspace token
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces: HTTP endpoint for listing workspaces visible to the caller
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug: HTTP endpoint for retrieving a single workspace by its slug
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens: HTTP endpoint for listing token metadata for the identified workspace
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces: HTTP endpoint for creating a new workspace; requires user API key authentication
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens: HTTP endpoint for creating a workspace token scoped to the identified workspace
glossary.SCP-style SSH: A git URL format matching git@<host>:<path>, e.g. git@github.com:org/repo.git
glossary.af_at_: Token prefix string identifying an admin token credential in the Authorization header
glossary.af_key_: Token prefix string identifying a user API key credential in the Authorization header
glossary.af_wt_: Token prefix string identifying a workspace token credential in the Authorization header
glossary.auth middleware: The server_foundation (spec 01) HTTP middleware layer that validates credentials, checks user block status, and attaches resolved identity and workspace_id to request context before handlers run
glossary.base62: An encoding alphabet using characters 0-9, A-Z, and a-z (62 total), used to generate token_id and secret values
glossary.branch: Optional target git branch for a workspace; null means the repository's default branch
glossary.created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp recording when a workspace or token record was inserted
glossary.crypto/rand: Go's cryptographically secure random byte source from the standard library, used for generating token_id and secret values
glossary.expires: Integer request field specifying token lifetime in days; must be 0, 30, 60, or 90; defaults to 30
glossary.expires_at: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when a workspace token expires; null means indefinite
glossary.git_url: A git remote URL accepted in HTTPS (https://) or SCP-style SSH (git@<host>:<path>) format
glossary.label: An optional human-readable name for a workspace token, stored as null if empty or omitted
glossary.owner_user_id: The user ID of the workspace creator and owner, stored in the workspaces table
glossary.revoked_at: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when a workspace token was permanently revoked; null means not revoked
glossary.secret: A 32-character base62 random string comprising the second component of a workspace token, stored only as a SHA-256 hash
glossary.slug: A globally unique, URL-safe workspace identifier (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 3-64 chars, starts with letter, no trailing hyphen)
glossary.team_id: An identifier referencing an owning team record in the teams table
glossary.teams: The database table (owned by server_foundation spec 01) storing team records and their active status
glossary.token_id: An 8-character base62 alphanumeric identifier uniquely identifying a workspace token within the workspace_tokens table
glossary.updated_at: ISO 8601 timestamp recording the last modification time of a workspace record; set equal to created_at on insert
glossary.workspace: A git-repo-scoped execution context in af-hub, identified by a globally unique slug
glossary.workspace token: A delegated, read-only credential scoped to a single workspace, formatted as af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>
glossary.workspace_id: The internal UUID identifying a workspace, attached to request context by the auth middleware when a workspace token is authenticated
glossary.workspace_tokens: The database table storing workspace token metadata including hashed secrets, expiry, and revocation timestamps
glossary.workspaces: The database table storing workspace records including slug, git_url, branch, team_id, and owner_user_id

user_story.goal: submit a workspace slug that conforms to naming rules
user_story.benefit: slugs are safe for use in URLs and globally unambiguous

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts a `slug` that is 3-64 characters long, starts with a lowercase letter, contains only lowercase alphanumeric characters or hyphens, and does not end with a hyphen
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: slug is stored in the `workspaces` table and returned in the workspace object

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `slug` fails any formatting rule (wrong length, wrong character set, starts with non-letter, ends with hyphen)

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 409 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `slug` already exists in the `workspaces` table

edge_cases.error_condition: the submitted `slug` is exactly 2 characters (below minimum) or exactly 65 characters (above maximum)
the submitted `slug` ends with a hyphen (e.g. "my-workspace-")
the submitted `slug` starts with a digit or hyphen rather than a lowercase letter

user_story.goal: provide a git remote URL in HTTPS or SCP-style SSH format
user_story.benefit: the system stores only valid, well-formed git remote URLs

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts a `git_url` that begins with `https://` or matches the SCP-style SSH pattern `git@<host>:<path>`, is at most 2048 characters, and stores it as-is without reachability validation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `git_url` is stored in the `workspaces` table and returned verbatim in the workspace object

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `git_url` does not start with `https://` and does not match the SCP-style SSH pattern (e.g. uses `ssh://`, `git://`, `http://`, or any other scheme)

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `git_url` exceeds 2048 characters

edge_cases.error_condition: the submitted `git_url` is exactly 2049 characters (one over the limit) but otherwise valid in scheme

edge_cases.action: accepts a `git_url` of exactly 2048 characters that starts with `https://`

user_story.benefit: the system records the intended branch with basic sanity checks without requiring full git-ref validation

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts `branch` as null or omitted, storing null to indicate the repository's default `branch`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `branch` field in returned workspace object is null

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts a non-null `branch` string of at most 255 characters containing no ASCII whitespace (space 0x20, tab 0x09, newline 0x0A, carriage return 0x0D) and stores it as-is
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `branch` stored verbatim and returned in workspace object

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `branch` exceeds 255 characters

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the submitted `branch` contains any ASCII whitespace character (space, tab, newline, or carriage return)

edge_cases.action: accepts or rejects based on the whitespace/length rules only; an empty string contains no whitespace and is within length, so it is stored as an empty string (not normalized to null unless the PRD specifies otherwise)
edge_cases.return_contract: `branch` stored as empty string or HTTP 400 if implementation treats empty string as invalid — implementation must document this choice
edge_cases.error_condition: the submitted `branch` is an empty string ""

requirements.title: Team ID validation on workspace creation

user_story.goal: optionally associate the workspace with an active team
user_story.benefit: workspaces can be group-owned, and the system catches invalid or inactive team references at creation time

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts `team_id` as null or omitted and stores null in the `workspaces` table
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `team_id` field in returned workspace object is null

acceptance_criteria.action: executes a direct SQL query against the `teams` table to verify the team exists and has active status; if valid, stores `team_id` in the `workspaces` record
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: workspace created with HTTP 201 and the provided `team_id` in the response
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a non-null `team_id` is provided in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the provided `team_id` does not exist in the `teams` table or exists but is not active

user_story.goal: create a new workspace via API
user_story.benefit: I become the owner and can manage the workspace and its tokens

acceptance_criteria.action: inserts a new record into the `workspaces` table with `owner_user_id` set to the authenticated user's ID, `updated_at` set equal to `created_at`, and returns the full workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with workspace object containing all fields defined in the workspace object schema
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid request authenticated with a user API key passes all validation checks

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with an admin token instead of a user API key

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a `workspace token` instead of a user API key

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with the standard JSON error envelope; no partial workspace record is left in a visible state
edge_cases.error_condition: an unexpected database error occurs during the INSERT into the `workspaces` table

user_story.goal: retrieve a list of workspaces I have access to
user_story.benefit: I can see the workspaces I own or, as admin, all workspaces in the system

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `workspaces` table for records where `owner_user_id` matches the authenticated user and returns them ordered by `created_at` ASC, with `id` ASC as tiebreaker
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a bare JSON array of workspace objects
acceptance_criteria.state: caller is authenticated with a user API key

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `workspaces` table for all records ordered by `created_at` ASC, with `id` ASC as tiebreaker, and returns all workspaces
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a bare JSON array of all workspace objects
acceptance_criteria.state: caller is authenticated with an admin token

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a `workspace token`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an empty JSON array
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the authenticated user owns no workspaces

user_story.role: workspace owner, admin, or workspace token holder
user_story.goal: retrieve full details of a specific workspace by its slug
user_story.benefit: I can inspect workspace configuration and metadata

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the full workspace object for the identified workspace
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with workspace object containing all fields defined in the workspace object schema
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the request is authenticated by a user API key belonging to the workspace owner or by an admin token and the `slug` exists in the `workspaces` table

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the full workspace object with all fields identical to what owner and admin callers receive
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with workspace object containing all fields defined in the workspace object schema; no fields are redacted
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the request is authenticated by a valid `workspace token` scoped to the workspace identified by `:slug`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope; no distinction is made between not found and not authorized
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `slug` does not exist in the `workspaces` table, regardless of caller type

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated by a user API key for a non-owner user and the `slug` exists

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated by a valid `workspace token` scoped to a different workspace than the one identified by `:slug`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 404 (not HTTP 403), preserving information hiding — the non-existence of the workspace is not treated as a secret
edge_cases.error_condition: a non-owner user queries a `slug` that does not exist in the `workspaces` table

user_story.goal: receive a consistent workspace object shape from all workspace endpoints
user_story.benefit: I can write a single deserialization path for workspace data regardless of which endpoint returned it

acceptance_criteria.action: returns workspace objects with exactly the following fields: `id` (UUID string), `slug` (string), `git_url` (string), `branch` (string or null), `team_id` (string or null), `owner_user_id` (string), `created_at` (ISO 8601), `updated_at` (ISO 8601)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON object with all eight fields present; no additional or omitted fields

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the same workspace object schema for all caller types (owner, admin, `workspace token`) without redacting any fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: identical JSON shape regardless of credential type used

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `updated_at` equal to `created_at` in the INSERT statement so that the returned workspace object reflects this equality on creation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `updated_at` equals `created_at` in the HTTP 201 response body

user_story.goal: create a delegated read-only token for a workspace
user_story.benefit: tools, agents, and programs can access the workspace without exposing my primary credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a `token_id` (8-char base62 via `crypto/rand`) and `secret` (32-char base62 via `crypto/rand`), stores the SHA-256 hash of the `secret` and token metadata in the `workspace_tokens` table, and returns the full token creation response including the plaintext token string exactly once
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with token creation response object containing `token`, `token_id`, `label`, `expires_at`, `created_at`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid request authenticated by the workspace owner's API key or an admin token is received for an existing workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: constructs the plaintext token string as `af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>` and returns it in the `token` field of the response; the plaintext `secret` is never written to the `workspace_tokens` table
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `token` field in response is the full string; `secret` column in `workspace_tokens` stores SHA-256 hash only

acceptance_criteria.action: defaults `expires` to 30 when the field is omitted from the request body, and computes `expires_at` as `created_at` plus the specified number of days; when `expires` is 0, stores null for `expires_at`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `expires_at` in response is null when `expires`=0, otherwise an ISO 8601 timestamp

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `expires` field value is not one of 0, 30, 60, or 90

acceptance_criteria.action: normalizes an empty string `label` to null before storing; accepts a null or omitted `label` as null; stores a non-empty `label` of at most 128 characters as-is
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: `label` in response is null when empty string or omitted; non-empty string otherwise

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 400 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `label` string exceeds 128 characters

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a `workspace token`, a non-owner user API key, or any credential without workspace ownership or admin rights

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:slug` does not exist in the `workspaces` table

edge_cases.action: silently retries `token_id` generation up to 3 times; if all 3 attempts result in collision, returns HTTP 500 with the standard JSON error envelope; no partial token record is inserted
edge_cases.error_condition: the generated `token_id` collides with an existing record in `workspace_tokens`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with the standard JSON error envelope; no partial token record is visible
edge_cases.error_condition: an unexpected database error occurs during the INSERT into `workspace_tokens` (not a collision)

edge_cases.action: does not enforce a maximum count of tokens per workspace; multiple tokens with the same `label` are permitted within the same workspace
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 201 regardless of how many tokens already exist for the workspace

user_story.goal: see all tokens associated with a workspace including expired and revoked ones
user_story.benefit: I can audit token usage and identify which tokens need attention

acceptance_criteria.action: queries `workspace_tokens` for all tokens scoped to the workspace, ordered by `created_at` ASC with `id` ASC as tiebreaker, and returns the metadata array including expired and revoked tokens
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with a bare JSON array of token metadata objects, each containing `token_id`, `label`, `created_at`, `expires_at`, `revoked_at`; the `secret` is never included
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid request authenticated by the workspace owner's API key or an admin token is received for an existing workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a `workspace token`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:slug` does not exist in the `workspaces` table

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a non-owner user API key

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an empty JSON array

user_story.goal: permanently revoke a specific workspace token
user_story.benefit: compromised or unnecessary tokens can be invalidated immediately

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `revoked_at` to the current timestamp in the `workspace_tokens` record and returns HTTP 204 with no response body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid request authenticated by the workspace owner's API key or an admin token is received and the `token_id` exists in `workspace_tokens` and belongs to the workspace identified by `:slug`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 204 with no response body (idempotent behavior)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `token_id` exists, belongs to the workspace identified by `:slug`, but `revoked_at` is already set (token was previously revoked)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `token_id` does not exist in `workspace_tokens` at all

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope (information hiding: cross-workspace token IDs are treated as not found)
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `token_id` exists in `workspace_tokens` but belongs to a different workspace than the one identified by `:slug`

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request is authenticated with a `workspace token`, a non-owner user API key, or any credential without workspace ownership or admin rights

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 and the standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:slug` does not exist in the `workspaces` table

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with the standard JSON error envelope; `revoked_at` remains unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: an unexpected database error occurs during the UPDATE to set `revoked_at`

user_story.goal: generate, store, and validate workspace tokens using cryptographically secure methods
user_story.benefit: tokens are unforgeable and their compromise does not expose the hashed secret stored in the database

acceptance_criteria.action: generates `token_id` as 8 characters from the `base62` alphabet (0-9A-Za-z) using `crypto/rand`, and `secret` as 32 characters from the same `base62` alphabet using `crypto/rand`, then assembles the full token string as `af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>`
stores the SHA-256 hash of the `secret` in the hashed-secret column of the `workspace_tokens` table; the plaintext `secret` is discarded after constructing the response
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: only the SHA-256 hash is persisted; plaintext is never written to the database

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 401 Unauthorized before the route handler runs
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 401 with standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.state: a request presents a `workspace token` with a non-null `revoked_at` or a non-null `expires_at` that is in the past

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 401 Unauthorized before the route handler runs
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 401 with standard JSON error envelope
acceptance_criteria.state: a request presents a `workspace token` belonging to a user whose account is blocked

edge_cases.action: the generator propagates the error as a Go error return value (does not call os.Exit or panic); the handler returns HTTP 500 to the caller
edge_cases.error_condition: the `crypto/rand` reader returns an error during `token_id` or `secret` generation

user_story.goal: enforce consistent permission rules across all workspace and token endpoints
user_story.benefit: callers are granted exactly the access they are entitled to and no more

acceptance_criteria.action: enforce access control according to the access matrix: admin tokens may call all endpoints except `POST /api/v1/workspaces`; user API keys may create workspaces and access their own workspaces and tokens; valid scoped `workspace token` credentials may only call `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug` for their own workspace; all other combinations return HTTP 403
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 for unauthorized combinations; appropriate success code for authorized ones

acceptance_criteria.action: compares the `workspace_id` attached to the request context by the `auth middleware` (for `workspace token` callers) against the workspace's `id` resolved from `:slug`; returns HTTP 403 if there is a mismatch
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with standard JSON error envelope on workspace_id mismatch

user_story.goal: send JSON request bodies to POST endpoints without strict Content-Type enforcement
user_story.benefit: clients are not broken by minor header omissions and can send extra fields safely

acceptance_criteria.action: parse request bodies as JSON using Echo's default JSON binding; silently ignore unknown or extra fields present in the request body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: no HTTP 400 is returned solely due to unknown fields in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: attempt JSON parsing regardless of the Content-Type header value; do not enforce Content-Type: application/json
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: request with no Content-Type header but valid JSON body is processed normally

correctness_properties.title: Workspace slug global uniqueness
correctness_properties.for_any: pair of workspace records in the `workspaces` table
correctness_properties.invariant: no two records share the same `slug` value; the unique index in the database enforces this at the storage layer, and the handler returns HTTP 409 on collision

correctness_properties.title: Workspace token secret never stored in plaintext
correctness_properties.invariant: the plaintext `secret` component of the token string is never written to the database; only the SHA-256 hash of the `secret` is stored

correctness_properties.title: Token plaintext returned exactly once
correctness_properties.invariant: the full plaintext token string (af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>) appears in exactly one HTTP response — the 201 response to the creation request — and is never returned by any subsequent endpoint

correctness_properties.title: Expired and revoked tokens rejected by middleware
correctness_properties.for_any: HTTP request presenting an expired or revoked `workspace token`
correctness_properties.invariant: the request is rejected with HTTP 401 by the `auth middleware` before any route handler executes, regardless of which endpoint is targeted

correctness_properties.title: workspace_id scope enforcement for workspace token callers
correctness_properties.for_any: request authenticated by a valid `workspace token` targeting GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug
correctness_properties.invariant: the handler compares the `workspace_id` attached to the request context against the workspace resolved from `:slug`; a mismatch always produces HTTP 403

correctness_properties.title: updated_at equals created_at on workspace creation
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record inserted into the `workspaces` table
correctness_properties.invariant: `updated_at` is set equal to `created_at` in the INSERT statement; no other value is ever stored for `updated_at` at creation time

correctness_properties.title: token_id collision retry bounded at 3
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of the workspace token creation handler
correctness_properties.invariant: `token_id` generation is retried at most 3 times on collision; the handler never enters an unbounded retry loop and returns HTTP 500 after 3 failed attempts

correctness_properties.title: Revoked token idempotency
correctness_properties.for_any: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id request where the token exists and belongs to the workspace
correctness_properties.invariant: the response is always HTTP 204 regardless of whether the token was already revoked, making revocation idempotent

correctness_properties.title: List ordering stability
correctness_properties.invariant: results are ordered by `created_at` ASC with `id` ASC as the tiebreaker, producing a stable, deterministic order

execution_paths.title: Create workspace with API key authentication

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/workspaces with Authorization: Bearer af_key_<key> header and JSON body containing slug, git_url, and optional branch and team_id
validates the `af_key_` prefixed credential, resolves the user identity, attaches it to the request context, and forwards the request to the handler
validates slug format, git_url format, branch constraints, and team_id existence (if provided) via direct SQL query against the `teams` table
inserts a new record into the `workspaces` table with owner_user_id set to the authenticated user's ID and updated_at equal to created_at
returns HTTP 201 with the full workspace object including all eight fields defined in the workspace object schema

execution_paths.title: Create workspace token and receive plaintext secret

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens with Authorization: Bearer af_key_<key> header and JSON body specifying optional label and expires
validates the `af_key_` credential, resolves the user identity, and attaches it to the request context
resolves the workspace from `:slug`, verifies the authenticated user is the owner or the credential is an admin token, and validates the label and expires fields
uses `crypto/rand` to generate an 8-char `base62` `token_id` and 32-char `base62` `secret`; computes SHA-256 hash of the `secret`
inserts a record into `workspace_tokens` with the hashed secret, label, expires_at, and created_at; if token_id collides, retries up to 3 times before returning HTTP 500
returns HTTP 201 with the token creation response containing the plaintext token string (af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>), token_id, label, expires_at, and created_at

execution_paths.title: Read workspace using workspace token (delegated access)

steps.action: sends GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug with Authorization: Bearer af_wt_<token_id>_<secret> header

detects the `af_wt_` prefix, looks up the token in `workspace_tokens` by token_id, verifies the SHA-256 hash of the presented secret matches the stored hash, checks that revoked_at is null, checks that expires_at is either null or in the future, checks that the owning user is not blocked, and attaches the resolved workspace_id to the request context
resolves the workspace from `:slug` in the `workspaces` table; if not found, returns HTTP 404
compares the workspace_id from request context with the resolved workspace's id; if mismatch, returns HTTP 403
returns HTTP 200 with the full workspace object containing all eight fields
sends DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id with Authorization: Bearer af_key_<key> header
validates the `af_key_` credential, resolves the user identity, and attaches it to the request context
resolves the workspace from `:slug`; verifies the authenticated user is the owner or admin; looks up the `token_id` in `workspace_tokens` scoped to this workspace_id

if token_id is not found or belongs to a different workspace, returns HTTP 404; if found (including already-revoked), sets revoked_at to current timestamp (or leaves existing revoked_at) and returns HTTP 204
sends GET /api/v1/workspaces with Authorization: Bearer af_at_<admin_token> header
validates the `af_at_` credential and attaches admin identity to the request context
queries the `workspaces` table for all records ordered by created_at ASC, id ASC and returns them as a bare JSON array

error_handling.condition: Slug fails format validation (wrong length, wrong characters, starts with non-letter, ends with hyphen)
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no database write occurs

error_handling.condition: Slug already exists in the `workspaces` table
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 409 with standard JSON error envelope; no new record is created

error_handling.condition: `git_url` uses an unsupported scheme (not https:// and not SCP-style SSH)
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no database write occurs

error_handling.condition: `git_url` exceeds 2048 characters
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no database write occurs

error_handling.condition: `branch` exceeds 255 characters or contains ASCII whitespace
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no database write occurs

error_handling.condition: `team_id` does not exist in the `teams` table or exists but is not active
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no workspace record is created

error_handling.condition: Admin token is used to call POST /api/v1/workspaces
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 403 with standard JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: `workspace token` is used to call GET /api/v1/workspaces
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 403 with standard JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: Non-owner user API key is used to call GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug for a workspace they do not own
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 403 with standard JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: Valid `workspace token` scoped to a different workspace is used to call GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 403 after comparing token's workspace_id against the resolved workspace's id

error_handling.condition: `:slug` does not exist in the `workspaces` table for any GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug request
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 404 with standard JSON error envelope regardless of caller type

error_handling.condition: `expires` value in token creation request is not one of 0, 30, 60, or 90
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no token is created

error_handling.condition: `label` string in token creation request exceeds 128 characters
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 400 with standard JSON error envelope; no token is created

error_handling.condition: All 3 `token_id` generation attempts result in collision with existing `workspace_tokens` records
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 500 with standard JSON error envelope; no token record is inserted

error_handling.condition: `token_id` in DELETE request does not exist in `workspace_tokens` or belongs to a different workspace than `:slug`
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 404 with standard JSON error envelope (information hiding; cross-workspace IDs treated as not found)

error_handling.condition: Expired or revoked `workspace token` is presented in the Authorization header
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 before any handler runs; the token is treated identically to an invalid credential

error_handling.condition: `workspace token` belonging to a blocked user is presented in the Authorization header
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 before any handler runs

error_handling.condition: `crypto/rand` returns an error during `token_id` or `secret` generation
error_handling.behavior: Generator propagates error as a Go error return value; handler returns HTTP 500 with standard JSON error envelope; no partial record is written

error_handling.condition: Unexpected database error during workspace INSERT
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 500 with standard JSON error envelope; no partial workspace record is visible

error_handling.condition: Unexpected database error during token UPDATE (revocation)
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 500 with standard JSON error envelope; `revoked_at` remains unchanged

symbols.name: Read
symbols.notes: Used to generate cryptographically secure random bytes for token_id (8 base62 chars) and secret (32 base62 chars). The number of raw bytes consumed is implementation-defined — sufficient bytes must be read to produce the required base62 output length.

symbols.name: Sum256
symbols.notes: Used to hash the plaintext secret before storing in the workspace_tokens table. The resulting [32]byte is stored in the hashed-secret column.

symbols.name: Context.Bind
symbols.notes: Used in POST handlers to parse JSON request bodies. Unknown fields are silently ignored per Echo's default behavior. Content-Type enforcement is not performed.

symbols.name: Context.JSON
symbols.signature: func (c echo.Context) JSON(code int, i interface{}) error
symbols.notes: Used to write JSON responses with HTTP status codes.

symbols.name: Context.NoContent
symbols.notes: Used by DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id to return HTTP 204 with no body.

symbols.name: Context.Param
symbols.notes: Used to extract :slug and :token_id path parameters from the request URL.
